mnemonics.title: PMLC Phases Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember the 5 phases as: I PLAN EXECUTE MONITOR CLOSE → Initiation, Planning, Execution, Monitoring & Controlling, Closure. Each word starts with letters in order: I-P-E-M-C.

mnemonics.title: Project Charter Contents
mnemonics.content: IPOC-D: Include Purpose, Objectives, Constraints, Deliverables, and authorize the manager to use resources.

mnemonics.title: WBS Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: START: Specify scope,分解 Decompose into components, Assign codes, Review for completeness, Finalize and Validate.

acronyms.usage: The framework of 5 phases a project goes through from start to finish
Hierarchical decomposition of project scope into manageable work packages
Matrix used to define roles and responsibilities in project tasks
Technical process of developing software (requirements to maintenance)
Project scheduling technique for analyzing and representing tasks
Algorithm for scheduling project activities and identifying critical path
Technique for measuring project performance and progress

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a timeline from left to right: INITIATE (light bulb icon) → PLAN (clipboard) → EXECUTE (gear) → MONITOR (radar/magnifier) → CLOSE (flag). Think of it as a journey from idea to celebration.

Draw an inverted tree or org-chart structure. At top: Project Name. Branches: Major deliverables. Sub-branches: Smaller components. Leaves: Work packages (smallest units). All branches roll up to the root.
Circle all tasks on your network diagram with ZERO float/slack. These connected tasks form the critical path - like the central spine of the project. Any break here delays everything.

2x2 matrix: X-axis = Interest (Low to High), Y-axis = Power/Influence (Low to High). High-High = Manage Closely, High-Power/Low-Interest = Keep Satisfied, Low-Power/High-Interest = Keep Informed, Low-Low = Monitor.

analogies.analogy: Like building a house: Initiation = deciding to build and getting permits; Planning = blueprints and material lists; Execution = actual construction; Monitoring = inspections and quality checks; Closure = moving in and final paperwork.

Just as a book's TOC breaks down chapters into sections and subsections, WBS breaks down the project into smaller, manageable pieces. Every piece in WBS should appear in the final 'book' (project deliverable).
Just as a marriage certificate formally establishes a marriage, the Project Charter formally establishes the project and gives the 'authority' (project manager) the right to use resources.

Think of project tasks as chain links. The critical path is the longest chain - it's the weakest link that determines how long the chain (project) will last. Strengthening other links doesn't help if the critical path isn't addressed.
